title: Running Sum of 1D Array
description: Given an array nums, return the running sum where runningSum[i] = sum(nums[0] to nums[i]).

title: Reverse Words in a String III
description: Given a string s, reverse the order of characters in each word within a sentence while still preserving whitespace and initial word order.
constraints:
1 ≤ s.length ≤ 5 * 10^4
s contains printable ASCII characters
s does not contain leading or trailing spaces
There is at least one word in s, and words are separated by a single space.
explanation: Each word is individually reversed while their positions remain.

title: Longest Subarray With At Most K Distinct Integers
description: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return the length of the longest subarray that contains at most k distinct integers.
explanation: The longest subarray with at most 2 distinct integers is [1,2,1,2] of length 4.
hints: Use a sliding window with two pointers.
Maintain a frequency map of elements in the window.
Shrink the window from the left when distinct count exceeds k.

title: Zigzag Level Order Traversal
description: Given the root of a binary tree, return the zigzag level order traversal of its nodes' values (i.e., from left to right, then right to left for the next level and alternate between).
constraints:
The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 2000]
-100 ≤ Node.val ≤ 100
explanation: Level 0 left-to-right: [3]. Level 1 right-to-left: [20,9]. Level 2 left-to-right: [15,7].
hints: Use BFS with a queue.
Track the current level's direction using a boolean flag.
Use a deque to efficiently add to front or back depending on direction.

title: Group Anagrams
description: Given an array of strings strs, group the anagrams together. You can return the answer in any order.
constraints:
1 ≤ strs.length ≤ 10^4
0 ≤ strs[i].length ≤ 100
strs[i] consists of lowercase English letters.
explanation: Strings that are anagrams share the same sorted character form. Group them by that key.
hints: Sort each string and use sorted form as a key.
Use a HashMap to map sorted keys to lists of original strings.
Iterate once through strs and populate the map.

title: Burst Balloons
description: You are given n balloons indexed from 0 to n-1. Each balloon is painted with a number on it represented by an array nums. You are asked to burst all the balloons. If you burst the ith balloon, you will get nums[i-1]*nums[i]*nums[i+1] coins. If i-1 or i+1 goes out of bounds, treat it as 1. Return the maximum coins you can collect by bursting the balloons wisely.
hints: Think in reverse: which balloon is burst LAST in a range?
Define dp[i][j] as max coins from bursting all balloons between i and j (exclusive).
For each possible last balloon k in range (i,j), dp[i][j] = max(dp[i][k] + nums[i]*nums[k]*nums[j] + dp[k][j]).
Pad the array with 1s at both ends.
Fill the DP table by increasing interval length.

title: Shortest Path in a Grid with Obstacles Elimination
description: You are given an m×n grid where each cell is either 0 (empty) or 1 (obstacle). In one step, you can move up, down, left, or right. You can eliminate at most k obstacles. Return the minimum number of steps to walk from (0,0) to (m-1,n-1), or -1 if not possible.
constraints:
m == grid.length
n == grid[i].length
1 ≤ m, n ≤ 40
1 ≤ k ≤ m*n
grid[i][j] == 0 or 1
explanation: The shortest path with at most 1 obstacle elimination takes 6 steps.
hints: Use BFS since we need shortest path.
State = (row, col, remaining_eliminations).
Use a 3D visited array visited[row][col][k_remaining].
Enqueue starting state (0,0,k).
When stepping on obstacle, decrement k; only enqueue if k>=0.

title: Word Search II
description: Given an m×n board of characters and a list of words, return all words on the board. Each word must be constructed from letters of sequentially adjacent cells (horizontally or vertically neighboring). The same letter cell may not be used more than once in a word.
constraints:
m == board.length
n == board[i].length
1 ≤ m, n ≤ 12
board[i][j] is a lowercase English letter
1 ≤ words.length ≤ 3 * 10^4
1 ≤ words[i].length ≤ 10
explanation: Both 'eat' and 'oath' can be traced on the board following adjacency rules.
hints: Build a Trie from all words.
DFS from each cell, following Trie nodes.
Mark cells visited during DFS, unmark on backtrack.
Prune Trie nodes after a word is found to avoid duplicates.
Use a $ or isEnd flag in Trie nodes to record found words.

title: Range Sum Query with Updates
description: Implement a data structure that supports: update(i, val) — update element at index i to val; sumRange(left, right) — return sum of elements from index left to right (inclusive). Multiple queries on a mutable array.
constraints:
1 ≤ nums.length ≤ 3 * 10^4
-100 ≤ nums[i] ≤ 100
0 ≤ left ≤ right < nums.length
At most 3 * 10^4 calls to update and sumRange
explanation: After update(1,2): nums=[1,2,5]. sumRange(0,2)=1+2+5=8.
hints: Build a segment tree with 2n nodes.
Each leaf stores original array value; internal node stores sum of children.
For update: walk from leaf to root updating sums.
For query: split range and sum relevant segments.
Segment tree gives O(log n) for both operations.

title: Expression Add Operators
description: Given a string num containing only digits and an integer target, return all possibilities to insert binary operators '+', '-', or '*' between the digits so that the resultant expression evaluates to the target value.
constraints:
1 ≤ num.length ≤ 10
num consists of only digits
-2^31 ≤ target ≤ 2^31 - 1
explanation: 1*2*3=6 and 1+2+3=6 both equal the target.
hints: Use DFS/backtracking, tracking current position, expression string, eval value, and last multiplicand.
Handle leading zeros: number starting with '0' must be just '0'.
For '*', new eval = eval - last + last * current (undo last add, re-multiply).
Pass the 'last' operand separately for multiplication correction.
Start recursion with an empty operator before first digit.

title: Delete Node in a Linked List
description: Given only access to a node (not the head) in a singly-linked list, delete that node. It is guaranteed the node is not the tail.
constraints:
The number of the nodes in the given list is in the range [2, 1000]
-1000 ≤ Node.val ≤ 1000
The value of each node in the list is unique
The node to be deleted is in the list and is not a tail node
explanation: Copy next node's value into current node, then skip next node.

title: Min Stack
description: Design a stack that supports push, pop, top, and retrieving the minimum element in constant time.
constraints:
-2^31 ≤ val ≤ 2^31 - 1
Methods pop, top, getMin operations will always be called on non-empty stacks
At most 3 * 10^4 calls will be made to push, pop, top, and getMin
sampleInput: push(-2), push(0), push(-3), getMin(), pop(), top(), getMin()
explanation: Maintain an auxiliary stack tracking minimums at each level.

title: Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array
description: Suppose an array of length n sorted in ascending order is rotated between 1 and n times. Given the rotated array nums of unique elements, return the minimum element of this array. Must run in O(log n) time.
constraints:
n == nums.length
1 ≤ n ≤ 5000
-5000 ≤ nums[i] ≤ 5000
All integers of nums are unique
nums is sorted and rotated between 1 and n times
explanation: The original array [1,2,3,4,5] was rotated 3 times; minimum is 1.
hints: Use binary search.
If nums[mid] > nums[right], minimum is in the right half.
Otherwise minimum is in the left half (including mid).

title: Number of Islands
description: Given an m×n 2D binary grid which represents a map of '1's (land) and '0's (water), return the number of islands. An island is surrounded by water and is formed by connecting adjacent lands horizontally or vertically.
constraints:
m == grid.length
n == grid[i].length
1 ≤ m, n ≤ 300
grid[i][j] is '0' or '1'
explanation: Two connected components of '1's exist: one 2-cell island and one 1-cell island.
hints: DFS or BFS from each unvisited '1'.
Mark visited cells to avoid recounting.
Each DFS/BFS call from a new '1' represents a new island.

title: Coin Change
description: You are given an integer array coins representing coins of different denominations and an integer amount representing a total amount of money. Return the fewest number of coins needed to make up that amount. If that amount cannot be made up, return -1.
explanation: Use coins 5+6=11, which requires 2 coins.
hints: dp[i] = minimum coins to make amount i.
Initialize dp[0]=0, rest as infinity.
For each amount, try all coins.

title: Palindrome Partitioning II
description: Given a string s, partition s such that every substring of the partition is a palindrome. Return the minimum cuts needed for a palindrome partitioning of s.
constraints:
1 ≤ s.length ≤ 2000
s consists of lowercase English letters only
explanation: One cut: "aa" | "b", both are palindromes.
hints: Precompute isPalin[i][j] using DP.
dp[i] = min cuts for s[0..i].
For each i, check all j<=i: if s[j..i] is palindrome, dp[i] = min(dp[i], dp[j-1]+1).
Base: if s[0..i] itself is palindrome, dp[i]=0.
Use expand-around-center to fill isPalin in O(n^2).

title: Number of Connected Components in Undirected Graph
description: You have n nodes labeled 0 to n-1 and a list of undirected edges. Write a function to find the number of connected components in the undirected graph.
constraints:
1 ≤ n ≤ 2000
1 ≤ edges.length ≤ 5000
edges[i].length == 2
0 ≤ a_i <= b_i < n
a_i != b_i
No repeated edges
explanation: Components: {0,1,2} and {3,4}.
hints: Initialize each node as its own parent.
For each edge, union the two endpoints.
Count distinct roots after all unions.
Path compression + union by rank for efficiency.
Count nodes where parent[i]==i.

title: Find Median from Data Stream
description: The MedianFinder class finds the median of a stream of integers. Implement addNum(int num) and findMedian() which returns the median of current data stream elements.
constraints:
-10^5 ≤ num ≤ 10^5
There will be at least one element before findMedian is called
At most 5 * 10^4 calls to addNum and findMedian
explanation: After [1,2], median=(1+2)/2=1.5. After [1,2,3], median=2.
hints: Use two heaps: max-heap for lower half, min-heap for upper half.
Keep heaps balanced (sizes differ by at most 1).
Median is top of max-heap or average of both tops.
Rebalance after each insertion.
Python: negate values for max-heap simulation.

title: Jump Game II
description: Given an integer array nums where nums[i] is the maximum jump length at position i. Return the minimum number of jumps to reach nums[n-1]. It is guaranteed you can reach nums[n-1].
explanation: Jump from index 0 to 1 (value 3), then to the last index. Minimum 2 jumps.
hints: Greedy: at each step, choose the farthest reachable position.
Track current jump boundary and next farthest reach.
Increment jump count when you reach current boundary.
No need to actually choose jump target, just track farthest.
O(n) solution is possible.

title: Maximum XOR of Two Numbers in an Array
description: Given an integer array nums, return the maximum result of nums[i] XOR nums[j], where 0 ≤ i ≤ j < n.
explanation: 25 XOR 5 = 11001 XOR 00101 = 11100 = 28, which is the maximum.
hints: Use a Trie of binary representations.
Insert each number bit by bit (MSB to LSB).
For each number, greedily choose the opposite bit at each level.
This gives the maximum XOR partner for each number.
Track max result across all numbers.

title: Move Zeroes
description: Given an integer array nums, move all 0s to the end while maintaining the relative order of the non-zero elements. Do this in-place.
explanation: Non-zeros 1,3,12 are moved to front; zeros fill the end.

title: Symmetric Tree
description: Given the root of a binary tree, check whether it is a mirror of itself (i.e., symmetric around its center).
constraints:
The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [1, 1000]
-100 ≤ Node.val ≤ 100
explanation: The left subtree is a mirror of the right subtree.

title: Clone Graph
description: Given a reference of a node in a connected undirected graph, return a deep copy (clone) of the graph. Each node contains a value and a list of neighbors.
constraints:
The number of nodes in the graph is in the range [0, 100]
1 ≤ Node.val ≤ 100
Node.val is unique for each node
There are no repeated edges and no self-loops
The graph is connected
explanation: A deep copy of the graph with the same adjacency structure.
hints: Use a HashMap from original node to its clone.
BFS or DFS from the given node.
When visiting a neighbor, if not yet cloned, create its clone and add to queue.

title: Decode String
description: Given an encoded string, return its decoded string. The encoding rule is: k[encoded_string] means the encoded_string inside square brackets is repeated exactly k times.
constraints:
1 ≤ s.length ≤ 30
s consists of lowercase English letters, digits, and square brackets '[]'
s is guaranteed to be a valid input
All the integers in s are in the range [1, 300]
explanation: 2[c] = cc, so 3[a2[c]] = 3[acc] = accaccacc.
hints: Use a stack to handle nesting.
Push current string and multiplier when '[' is encountered.
When ']' is encountered, pop and repeat the string.
Build number digit by digit before '['.

title: Subarray Sum Equals K
description: Given an array of integers nums and an integer k, return the total number of subarrays whose sum equals to k.
explanation: Subarrays [1,1] at indices [0,1] and [1,2] both sum to 2.
hints: Use prefix sum with a HashMap.
If prefix[j] - prefix[i] == k, then subarray (i,j] sums to k.
Store count of each prefix sum seen so far.
For each position, check if (currentSum - k) exists in the map.

title: Interleaving String
description: Given strings s1, s2, and s3, find whether s3 is formed by an interleaving of s1 and s2. Interleaving means combining s1 and s2 by selecting characters alternately while preserving relative order.
constraints:
0 ≤ s1.length, s2.length ≤ 100
0 ≤ s3.length ≤ 200
s1, s2, and s3 consist of lowercase English letters
explanation: s3 can be formed by interleaving s1 and s2.
hints: Define dp[i][j] = true if s3[0..i+j-1] is an interleaving of s1[0..i-1] and s2[0..j-1].
Base case: dp[0][0] = true.
First row and column handle cases where one string is empty.
O(m*n) time and space, space optimizable to O(n).

title: Critical Connections in a Network
description: There are n servers numbered 0 to n-1 connected by undirected server-to-server connections. A critical connection is a connection that, if removed, will make some servers unable to reach some other server. Return all critical connections in any order.
constraints:
2 ≤ n ≤ 10^5
n - 1 ≤ connections.length ≤ 10^5
0 ≤ a_i, b_i < n
a_i != b_i
No repeated connections
explanation: Connection [1,3] is a bridge; removing it disconnects server 3.
hints: Use Tarjan's bridge-finding algorithm.
Assign discovery times and low values via DFS.
An edge (u,v) is a bridge if low[v] > disc[u].
low[v] = min(disc[v], low of children, disc of back-edge ancestors).
Skip the parent edge during DFS traversal.

title: N-Queens II
description: The n-queens puzzle is the problem of placing n queens on an n×n chessboard such that no two queens attack each other. Given an integer n, return the number of distinct solutions to the n-queens puzzle.
explanation: There are two distinct solutions for the 4-queens problem.
hints: Use backtracking row by row.
Track which columns and diagonals are occupied.
Diagonals: use sets for (row-col) and (row+col).
When a valid placement for a row is found, recurse to next row.
Backtrack by removing queen and trying next column.

title: Count of Smaller Numbers After Self
description: Given an integer array nums, return an integer array counts where counts[i] is the number of smaller elements to the right of nums[i].
explanation: 5 has [2,1] smaller to its right (count=2). 2 has [1] (count=1). 6 has [1] (count=1). 1 has [] (count=0).
hints: Process from right to left.
Use a Fenwick (BIT) tree or Segment tree on coordinate-compressed values.
For each element, query sum of elements less than it already inserted.
Then insert the element into the BIT.
Coordinate compress to handle negative values.

title: Rearrange String k Distance Apart
description: Given a string s and an integer k, rearrange the string such that the same characters are at least k distance apart. Return any valid rearrangement or an empty string if not possible.
constraints:
1 ≤ s.length ≤ 3 * 10^5
s consists of lowercase English letters
1 ≤ k ≤ s.length
explanation: Each character appears at distance 3 from its next occurrence.
hints: Use a max-heap based on character frequency.
At each step, greedily pick the most frequent character.
After placing a character, cool it down for k steps.
Use a queue to track cooled-down characters.
If heap is empty but queue is non-empty, return empty string.

title: Pascal's Triangle
description: Given an integer numRows, return the first numRows of Pascal's triangle.
explanation: Each number is the sum of the two numbers directly above it.

title: Valid Anagram
description: Given two strings s and t, return true if t is an anagram of s, and false otherwise.
constraints:
1 ≤ s.length, t.length ≤ 5 * 10^4
s and t consist of lowercase English letters
explanation: Both strings contain the same characters with same frequencies.

title: Kth Smallest Element in a BST
description: Given the root of a binary search tree and an integer k, return the kth smallest value (1-indexed) of all the values of the nodes in the tree.
constraints:
The number of nodes in the tree is n
1 ≤ k ≤ n ≤ 10^4
0 ≤ Node.val ≤ 10^4
explanation: Inorder traversal of BST gives sorted order; 1st element is 1.
hints: Inorder traversal (left, root, right) of BST gives sorted values.
Count nodes during traversal.
Return the kth visited node's value.

title: Spiral Matrix
description: Given an m×n matrix, return all elements of the matrix in spiral order.
constraints:
m == matrix.length
n == matrix[i].length
1 ≤ m, n ≤ 10
-100 ≤ matrix[i][j] ≤ 100
explanation: Traverse right, down, left, up repeatedly, shrinking boundaries.
hints: Maintain top, bottom, left, right boundaries.
Traverse right across top row, then down right column, then left across bottom, then up left column.
After each pass, shrink the corresponding boundary.
Check boundary validity before each pass.

title: Container With Most Water
description: You are given an integer array height of length n. There are n vertical lines drawn at each index i of height[i]. Find two lines that together with the x-axis form a container that contains the most water.
explanation: Lines at index 1 and 8 (heights 8 and 7) form area = min(8,7)*(8-1) = 49.
hints: Use two pointers at both ends.
Area = min(height[left], height[right]) * (right - left).
Move the pointer with smaller height inward.
Update max area at each step.

title: Regular Expression Matching
description: Given an input string s and a pattern p, implement regular expression matching with support for '.' and '*'. '.' matches any single character. '*' matches zero or more of the preceding element.
constraints:
1 ≤ s.length ≤ 20
1 ≤ p.length ≤ 30
s contains only lowercase English letters
p contains only lowercase English letters, '.', and '*'
It is guaranteed that for each occurrence of '*', there will be a previous valid character to match
explanation: c* matches empty, a* matches 'aa', b matches 'b'.
hints: dp[i][j] = whether s[0..i-1] matches p[0..j-1].
If p[j-1]=='*': dp[i][j] = dp[i][j-2] (zero matches) or dp[i-1][j] if s[i-1] matches p[j-2].
Base: dp[0][j] handles patterns like a*b*c* that match empty string.
Fill table row by row.

title: Alien Dictionary
description: There is a new alien language that uses the English alphabet. You are given a list of strings words from the alien language's dictionary, where the strings in words are sorted lexicographically by the rules of this new language. Derive the order of letters in this alien language.
constraints:
1 ≤ words.length ≤ 100
1 ≤ words[i].length ≤ 100
words[i] consists of only lowercase English letters
All characters in words[i] are unique
explanation: t<f, w<e, r<t, e<r gives ordering wertf.
hints: Compare adjacent words to extract ordering constraints.
Build a directed graph of character orderings.
Topological sort (BFS/Kahn's) to find the order.
If cycle exists, return empty string (invalid).
If a shorter word comes after a longer word with same prefix, return empty.

title: Design Search Autocomplete System
description: Design a search autocomplete system for a search engine. Users may input a sentence (at least one word and end with a special character '#'). For each character they type except '#', you need to return the top 3 historical hot sentences that have the same prefix as the part of the sentence already typed. Hot degree is the number of times a user typed the exact sentence. If several sentences have the same hot degree, use ASCII-code order.
constraints:
n == sentences.length
n == times.length
1 ≤ n ≤ 100
1 ≤ sentences[i].length ≤ 100
1 ≤ times[i] ≤ 50
c is a lowercase English letter, a hash '#', or a space ' '
explanation: All sentences start with 'i'; sorted by frequency then lexicographically.
hints: Use a Trie where each node stores matching sentences with frequencies.
On '#', store the complete sentence to Trie and reset current input.
For each character, traverse the Trie and collect top-3 candidates.
At each Trie node, maintain a sorted list or use a min-heap of size 3.
Store (frequency, sentence) pairs and sort by (-freq, sentence) lexicographically.

title: Sudoku Solver
description: Write a program to solve a Sudoku puzzle by filling the empty cells. A sudoku solution must satisfy: each of the digits 1-9 must occur exactly once in each row, column, and each of the 3×3 sub-boxes.
constraints:
board.length == 9
board[i].length == 9
board[i][j] is a digit or '.'
explanation: Fill empty cells to satisfy all Sudoku constraints.
hints: Try placing digits 1-9 in each empty cell.
Check row, column, and 3x3 box validity before placing.
Recurse to next empty cell.
If no valid digit, backtrack.
Pre-compute sets of used digits per row/col/box for O(1) validity check.

title: The Skyline Problem
description: A city's skyline is the outer contour of the silhouette formed by all buildings when viewed from a distance. Given the buildings array where buildings[i]=[left_i, right_i, height_i], return the skyline formed by these buildings.
constraints:
1 ≤ buildings.length ≤ 10^4
0 ≤ left_i < right_i ≤ 2^31 - 1
1 ≤ height_i ≤ 2^31 - 1
buildings is sorted by left_i
explanation: Key points where the maximum height changes define the skyline.
hints: Create events: building start (negative height) and end.
Sort events by x-coordinate, breaking ties appropriately.
Use a max-heap to track active building heights.
At each event, update heap; if max height changes, record key point.
Remove ended buildings lazily from heap.

title: Find All Numbers Disappeared in an Array
description: Given an array nums of n integers where nums[i] is in the range [1, n], return an array of all the integers in the range [1, n] that do not appear in nums.
explanation: Numbers 5 and 6 are missing from the array.

title: Palindrome Linked List
description: Given the head of a singly linked list, return true if it is a palindrome or false otherwise.
constraints:
The number of nodes in the list is in the range [1, 10^5]
0 ≤ Node.val ≤ 9
explanation: The list reads the same forward and backward.

title: Longest Increasing Subsequence
description: Given an integer array nums, return the length of the longest strictly increasing subsequence.
explanation: [2,3,7,101] is the longest increasing subsequence of length 4.
hints: dp[i] = length of LIS ending at index i.
For each i, check all j<i where nums[j]<nums[i].
dp[i] = max(dp[j]+1) for all valid j.
O(n log n) solution using patience sorting + binary search is also possible.

title: Task Scheduler
description: Given a list of tasks with their frequencies and a cooldown interval n, return the least number of intervals the CPU will take to finish all the given tasks (idle intervals may be inserted).
constraints:
1 ≤ tasks.length ≤ 10^4
tasks[i] is uppercase English letter
0 ≤ n ≤ 100
explanation: A->B->idle->A->B->idle->A->B takes 8 intervals.
hints: Count frequency of each task.
Use max-heap to always schedule most frequent task first.
After each round of n+1 slots, refill heap with remaining tasks.
Calculate max slots needed: (maxFreq-1)*(n+1) + countOfMaxFreqTasks.
Answer is max(tasks.length, formula above).

title: Course Schedule II
description: There are a total of numCourses courses you have to take, labeled from 0 to numCourses-1. Given an array prerequisites where prerequisites[i]=[a,b] means you must take b before a, return the ordering of courses you should take to finish all courses. If impossible, return empty array.
explanation: Topological ordering where prerequisites come before courses.
hints: Build adjacency list and in-degree array.
BFS/Kahn's: start with all 0-indegree nodes.
Process nodes, reduce neighbor in-degrees.
If result length < numCourses, a cycle exists.
DFS with white-gray-black coloring also works.

title: Wildcard Matching
description: Given an input string s and a pattern p, implement wildcard pattern matching with support for '?' and '*'. '?' matches any single character. '*' matches any sequence of characters (including empty).
constraints:
0 ≤ s.length, p.length ≤ 2000
s contains only lowercase English letters
p contains only lowercase English letters, '?', or '*'
explanation: The first '*' matches empty, 'a' matches 'a', second '*' matches 'dce', 'b' matches 'b'.
hints: dp[i][j] = whether s[0..i-1] matches p[0..j-1].
If p[j-1]=='*': dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j] (match one char) or dp[i][j-1] (match empty).
Base: dp[0][0]=true; dp[0][j]=true only if p[0..j-1] are all '*'.
Two-pointer greedy approach is also possible.

title: Minimum Number of Arrows to Burst Balloons
description: There are some spherical balloons taped onto a flat wall. The balloons are represented as a 2D integer array points where points[i]=[x_start, x_end]. An arrow can be shot up at x=m and bursts all balloons with x_start ≤ m ≤ x_end. Return the minimum number of arrows needed.
explanation: Two arrows at x=6 and x=11 burst all balloons.
hints: Sort balloons by end coordinate.
Greedily shoot arrow at the end of current balloon.
An arrow bursts all balloons whose start ≤ arrow position.
Move to next unbursted balloon after each shot.
Count total shots needed.

title: Accounts Merge
description: Given a list of accounts where each account is a list of strings (first element is name, rest are emails), merge accounts that share at least one email. Return merged accounts in any order.
constraints:
1 ≤ accounts.length ≤ 1000
2 ≤ accounts[i].length ≤ 10
1 ≤ accounts[i][j].length ≤ 30
accounts[i][0] is the name of the account
explanation: Both accounts share b@x.com, so they are merged.
hints: Map each email to its account index using Union-Find.
Union all emails in the same account together.
Group emails by their root representative.
Attach account name to each merged group.
Sort emails within each merged account.

title: Single Number III
description: Given an integer array nums, in which exactly two elements appear only once and all the other elements appear exactly twice, find the two elements that appear only once. Return the answer in any order in O(n) time and O(1) extra space.
constraints:
2 ≤ nums.length ≤ 3 * 10^4
-2^31 ≤ nums[i] ≤ 2^31 - 1
Each integer in nums will appear twice, only two integers will appear once
explanation: 3 and 5 each appear once; all others appear twice.
hints: XOR all numbers to get xor = a^b (the two unique numbers).
Find any set bit in xor (lowest set bit: xor & -xor).
Divide numbers into two groups by that bit.
XOR each group separately to get each unique number.
Each pair cancels out within its group.

title: Minimum Window Substring
description: Given two strings s and t of lengths m and n respectively, return the minimum window substring of s such that every character in t (including duplicates) is included in the window. If no such substring exists, return empty string.
constraints:
m == s.length
n == t.length
1 ≤ m, n ≤ 10^5
s and t consist of uppercase and lowercase English letters
explanation: The minimum window containing A, B, C is 'BANC'.
hints: Use sliding window with two pointers.
Expand right until all chars of t are covered.
Shrink left while still covered to minimize window.
Track char frequencies using two maps.
Record minimum window whenever valid.

title: Isomorphic Strings
description: Given two strings s and t, determine if they are isomorphic. Two strings s and t are isomorphic if the characters in s can be replaced to get t. All occurrences of a character must be replaced with another character while preserving the order.
constraints:
1 ≤ s.length ≤ 5 * 10^4
t.length == s.length
s and t consist of any valid ASCII character
explanation: e→a, g→d. Mapping is consistent and bijective.

title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
description: You are given an array prices where prices[i] is the price of a given stock on the ith day. You want to maximize your profit by choosing a single day to buy and a single day to sell (after the buy day). Return the maximum profit achievable. If no profit, return 0.
explanation: Buy at 1, sell at 6 for profit 5.

title: Evaluate Division
description: You are given equations in the format A/B=k, and some queries. Return the answers. If answer does not exist, return -1.0.
explanation: a/b=2, b/c=3, so a/c=6.
hints: Build a weighted directed graph: A→B with weight k, B→A with weight 1/k.
For each query, do DFS/BFS from source to destination multiplying edge weights.
If no path exists, return -1.0.
Handle query where both variables are equal (return 1.0 if known).

title: Maximum Average Subarray I
description: You are given an integer array nums consisting of n elements, and an integer k. Find a contiguous subarray of length k with the maximum average value and return this value.
explanation: Subarray [12,-5,-6,50] has sum 51 and average 51/4=12.75.
hints: Compute sum of first k elements.
Slide window: subtract leftmost, add new rightmost.
Track maximum sum seen.
Return max_sum / k.

title: Add Two Numbers II
description: You are given two non-empty linked lists representing two non-negative integers. The most significant digit comes first. Return their sum as a linked list in the same format.
constraints:
The number of nodes in each linked list is in the range [1, 100]
0 ≤ Node.val ≤ 9
Both non-negative
No leading zeros except the number 0 itself
hints: Use stacks to process from least significant digit.
Pop from both stacks, add digits and carry.
Build result list from back to front.
Handle remaining carry after both stacks are empty.

title: Minimum Cost to Cut a Stick
description: Given a wooden stick of length n units. The stick has labels from 0 to n. Given cuts array where you can perform cuts in any order, each cut has cost equal to current stick length. Return the minimum total cost of the cuts.
constraints:
2 ≤ n ≤ 10^6
1 ≤ cuts.length ≤ min(n-1, 100)
1 ≤ cuts[i] ≤ n-1
All cuts values are distinct
explanation: Optimal cut order yields total cost 16.
hints: Sort cuts and add endpoints 0 and n.
dp[i][j] = min cost to cut segment between cuts[i] and cuts[j].
Try each cut k between i and j as the first cut.
dp[i][j] = min(dp[i][k]+dp[k][j]) + (cuts[j]-cuts[i]).
Fill by increasing interval length.

title: Reconstruct Itinerary
description: You are given a list of airline tickets represented by pairs of departure and arrival airports [from, to]. Reconstruct the itinerary in order. Use all the tickets exactly once. The itinerary must begin with 'JFK'. If multiple valid itineraries exist, return the one with the smallest lexical order.
constraints:
1 ≤ tickets.length ≤ 300
tickets[i].length == 2
from_i and to_i consist of uppercase English letters
from_i != to_i
All the tickets belong to a man who departs from 'JFK'
explanation: Eulerian path using all tickets exactly once starting from JFK.
hints: Sort destination lists lexicographically for each airport.
Use Hierholzer's algorithm for Eulerian path.
DFS, choosing smallest destination first.
Add airport to result after all its outgoing edges are used.
Reverse the result at the end.

title: IPO
description: You need to maximize capital to invest in projects. You have initial capital w and can do at most k projects. Project i requires capital[i] to start and gives profits[i]. Each project can only be done once. Return the maximum capital after k projects.
constraints:
1 ≤ k ≤ 10^5
0 ≤ w ≤ 10^9
n == profits.length
n == capital.length
1 ≤ n ≤ 10^5
0 ≤ profits[i] ≤ 10^4
0 ≤ capital[i] ≤ 10^9
explanation: Do project 0 (profit 1, capital 0 needed). Now w=1. Do project 2 (profit 3). w=4.
hints: Sort projects by required capital.
Use a max-heap on profits for affordable projects.
For each of k iterations, push all affordable projects to heap.
Pop max profit from heap and add to w.
Repeat k times or until no affordable projects remain.

title: Permutation Sequence
description: The set [1, 2, 3, ..., n] contains a total of n! unique permutations. Given n and k, return the kth permutation sequence.
explanation: Permutations in order: 123, 132, 213, 231, 312, 321. 3rd is 213.
hints: Precompute factorials.
For each position, determine which digit goes there by (k-1)/factorial.
Remove used digit from available list.
Update k = (k-1) % factorial.
Repeat until all positions filled.

title: My Calendar III
description: Implement a MyCalendarThree class that records events and returns the maximum k-booking. An event is represented by a time range [start, end). k-booking means k events have some non-empty intersection.
constraints:
0 ≤ start < end ≤ 10^9
At most 400 calls will be made to book
explanation: After 3rd booking, maximum overlap becomes 2.
hints: Use a segment tree with lazy propagation on compressed coordinates.
Alternatively, use a difference array / line sweep with a sorted map.
For each booking [s,e]: increment at s, decrement at e.
Query prefix max after each insertion.
TreeMap in Java or SortedDict in Python works well.

title: Count and Say
description: The count-and-say sequence starts with '1'. Each term is generated by describing the previous term. Return the nth term.
explanation: 1→11→21→1211. Count and describe groups of same digits.

title: Squares of a Sorted Array
description: Given an integer array nums sorted in non-decreasing order, return an array of the squares of each number sorted in non-decreasing order.
constraints:
1 ≤ nums.length ≤ 10^4
-10^4 ≤ nums[i] ≤ 10^4
nums is sorted in non-decreasing order

title: Find the Duplicate Number
description: Given an array of integers nums containing n+1 integers where each integer is in the range [1, n] inclusive, prove that at least one duplicate must exist. Find the duplicate number without modifying the array, using only O(1) extra space.
constraints:
1 ≤ n ≤ 10^5
nums.length == n + 1
1 ≤ nums[i] ≤ n
All the integers in nums appear only once except for one integer which appears two or more times
explanation: 2 appears twice in the array.
hints: Treat array as linked list: index → nums[index].
Use Floyd's cycle detection (slow/fast pointers).
Phase 1: find meeting point inside cycle. Phase 2: find cycle entrance.

title: Construct Binary Tree from Preorder and Inorder Traversal
description: Given two integer arrays preorder and inorder where preorder is the preorder traversal and inorder is the inorder traversal of the same tree, construct and return the binary tree.
constraints:
1 ≤ preorder.length ≤ 3000
inorder.length == preorder.length
-3000 ≤ preorder[i], inorder[i] ≤ 3000
All values are unique
explanation: Root is preorder[0]=3. Find 3 in inorder; left has [9], right has [15,20,7].
hints: First element of preorder is always the root.
Find root's index in inorder to split left and right subtrees.
Recurse for left and right subtrees.
Use a HashMap for O(1) inorder index lookups.

title: Non-overlapping Intervals
description: Given an array of intervals intervals where intervals[i]=[start_i, end_i], return the minimum number of intervals you need to remove to make the rest of the intervals non-overlapping.
explanation: Remove [1,3] to make the rest non-overlapping.
hints: Sort by end time.
Greedily keep interval with smallest end time.
If current interval overlaps with last kept, increment removal count.
Otherwise, update last kept end time.
Answer = total - kept.

title: Substring with Concatenation of All Words
description: You are given a string s and an array of strings words. All words are of the same length. Return the starting indices of all substrings of s that is a concatenation of each word in words exactly once, in any order.
constraints:
1 ≤ s.length ≤ 10^4
1 ≤ words.length ≤ 5000
1 ≤ words[i].length ≤ 30
words[i] consists of lowercase English letters
s consists of lowercase English letters
explanation: Substrings at index 0 ('barfoo') and 9 ('foobar') are valid.
hints: Use a sliding window of total words length.
For each starting position 0 to wordLen-1, slide window by wordLen.
Maintain a frequency map of words in current window.
If window count matches required, record start index.
Slide by adding new word and removing old word.

title: Stone Game IV
description: Alice and Bob take turns removing stones. Alice goes first. Each player must remove a positive square number of stones. The player who cannot make a move loses. Given n stones, return true if Alice wins with optimal play.
explanation: No matter what Alice plays, Bob can always win with optimal strategy.
hints: dp[i] = whether the current player wins with i stones.
dp[0] = false (no move possible).
dp[i] = true if any dp[i-k^2] is false (opponent loses).
Precompute all perfect squares up to n.
dp[i] = OR over all squares k^2 ≤ i of NOT dp[i-k^2].

title: Minimum Cost to Reach Destination in Time
description: There is a country with n cities numbered 0 to n-1, connected by bidirectional roads with travel time. You have maxTime to reach city n-1 from city 0. Each city has a passing fee. Return the minimum fee to reach n-1 within maxTime, or -1 if impossible.
explanation: Path 0→1→2→5 costs 5+1+2+3=11 and takes 30 time units.
hints: dp[t][v] = minimum cost to reach city v at time exactly t.
Initialize dp[0][0] = passingFees[0], rest = infinity.
For each time step, relax edges.
dp[t][v] = min(dp[t-time][u] + passingFees[v]) for all edges (u,v).
Answer = min(dp[t][n-1]) for t in [1, maxTime].

title: Palindrome Partitioning
description: Given a string s, partition s such that every substring of the partition is a palindrome. Return all possible palindrome partitioning of s.
constraints:
1 ≤ s.length ≤ 16
s consists of lowercase English letters
explanation: Both partitions have all palindromic substrings.
hints: Use backtracking with DFS.
At each position, try all palindromic substrings starting there.
Precompute isPalin[i][j] for O(1) check.
Add current palindrome to path and recurse from next position.
Backtrack after each recursive call.

title: Stream of Characters
description: Implement the StreamChecker class: given a list of words, for each letter queried with query(letter), return true if some suffix of the letters queried so far spells a word from the words list.
constraints:
1 ≤ words.length ≤ 2000
1 ≤ words[i].length ≤ 2000
words[i] consists of lowercase English letters
letter is a lowercase English letter
At most 4 * 10^4 calls will be made to query
explanation: At 'd', suffix 'cd' matches. At 'f', single char 'f' matches.
hints: Build a Trie of reversed words.
Maintain a stream buffer of recent characters.
On each query, check if any suffix of current stream matches a word.
Traverse reversed stream against Trie.
Limit stream buffer to max word length for efficiency.

title: Roman to Integer
description: Given a roman numeral, convert it to an integer.
constraints:
1 ≤ s.length ≤ 15
s contains only the characters ('I','V','X','L','C','D','M')
It is guaranteed that s is a valid roman numeral in the range [1, 3999]

title: Maximum Depth of Binary Tree
description: Given the root of a binary tree, return its maximum depth (number of nodes along the longest path from root to farthest leaf).
constraints:
The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 10^4]
-100 ≤ Node.val ≤ 100
explanation: Longest path: 3→20→15 or 3→20→7, depth=3.

title: Product of Array Except Self
description: Given an integer array nums, return an array answer such that answer[i] is equal to the product of all elements of nums except nums[i]. Must run in O(n) without using division.
constraints:
2 ≤ nums.length ≤ 10^5
-30 ≤ nums[i] ≤ 30
The product of any prefix or suffix of nums is guaranteed to fit in a 32-bit integer
hints: Compute prefix products from left.
Compute suffix products from right.
answer[i] = prefix[i-1] * suffix[i+1].
Can be done in O(1) extra space by storing prefix in result and traversing right for suffix.

title: Daily Temperatures
description: Given an array of integers temperatures representing daily temperatures, return an array answer where answer[i] is the number of days you have to wait after the ith day to get a warmer temperature. If no future warmer day exists, keep 0.
explanation: After day 0 (73°), next warmer day is day 1 (74°), so answer[0]=1.
hints: Use a monotonic stack (decreasing).
Store indices of temperatures in the stack.
When current temp > stack top, pop and compute difference.
Remaining elements in stack have answer 0.

title: Pacific Atlantic Water Flow
description: There is an m×n rectangular island that borders both the Pacific and Atlantic oceans. Given an m×n integer matrix heights where heights[r][c] represents the height above sea level at cell (r,c). Return a list of grid coordinates where rain water can flow to both the Pacific and Atlantic oceans.
constraints:
m == heights.length
n == heights[r].length
1 ≤ m, n ≤ 200
0 ≤ heights[r][c] ≤ 10^5
explanation: These cells can drain to both Pacific (top/left) and Atlantic (bottom/right).
hints: Reverse flow: BFS/DFS from ocean borders inward.
Pacific borders: top row and left column.
Atlantic borders: bottom row and right column.
Cell is reachable if neighbor height ≥ current height (water flows in reverse).
Intersection of both reachable sets is the answer.

title: Distinct Subsequences
description: Given two strings s and t, return the number of distinct subsequences of s which equals t.
constraints:
1 ≤ s.length, t.length ≤ 1000
s and t consist of English letters
explanation: There are 3 ways to choose 'rabbit' as a subsequence from 'rabbbit'.
hints: dp[i][j] = # ways s[0..i-1] contains t[0..j-1] as subsequence.
Otherwise: dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j].
Base: dp[i][0] = 1 (empty t is always matched), dp[0][j>0] = 0.
Fill row by row.

title: Candy
description: There are n children standing in a line. Each child is assigned a rating value in ratings[i]. Give each child at least one candy. Children with higher ratings than adjacent children must receive more candies. Return the minimum number of candies.
explanation: Give [2,1,2] candies: child 0 gets 2 (higher than child 1), child 2 gets 2 (higher than child 1). Total=5.
hints: Two pass approach.
Left pass: if ratings[i] > ratings[i-1], candies[i] = candies[i-1]+1.
Right pass: if ratings[i] > ratings[i+1], candies[i] = max(candies[i], candies[i+1]+1).
Sum all candies.
Each child starts with 1 candy.

title: Redundant Connection II
description: In a directed graph with n nodes (labeled 1 to n) where each node has exactly one parent (like a tree with extra edge), find the edge that can be removed to make the graph a valid rooted tree. There may be a node with two parents or a cycle.
constraints:
n == edges.length
3 ≤ n ≤ 1000
edges[i].length == 2
1 ≤ u_i, v_i ≤ n
explanation: Removing [2,3] makes the graph a valid rooted tree.
hints: Case 1: a node has two parents — one of those edges is redundant.
Case 2: there is a cycle — the edge forming the cycle is redundant.
Case 3: both (node with 2 parents AND in a cycle) — remove the parent edge that is in the cycle.
Use Union-Find to detect cycles.
Track each node's parent; if parent already assigned, record two candidate edges.

title: Counting Bits
description: Given an integer n, return an array ans of length n+1 such that for each i (0 ≤ i ≤ n), ans[i] is the number of 1's in the binary representation of i.
hints: Shifting right removes last bit; add 1 if last bit was set.
O(n) time and space.
No need for built-in popcount.
Build array iteratively from 1 to n.

title: Combination Sum II
description: Given a collection of candidate numbers candidates and a target number target, find all unique combinations in candidates where the candidate numbers sum to target. Each number in candidates may only be used once in the combination. The solution set must not contain duplicate combinations.
explanation: All unique combinations that sum to 8.
hints: Sort candidates first to handle duplicates.
Use backtracking with a start index.
Skip duplicate candidates at same recursion level: if i>start && candidates[i]==candidates[i-1], skip.
Recurse with i+1 (not i) to prevent reuse.
Prune if current candidate > remaining target.

title: Number of Recent Calls
description: Implement the RecentCounter class that counts the number of recent requests within a certain time frame. ping(t) returns the number of requests made in the past 3000 milliseconds (including the new request). t is strictly increasing.
constraints:
1 ≤ t ≤ 10^9
Each test case will call ping with strictly increasing values of t
At most 10^4 calls will be made to ping

title: Third Maximum Number
description: Given an integer array nums, return the third distinct maximum number in this array. If the third maximum does not exist, return the maximum number.
explanation: Third distinct maximum is 1.

title: Range Sum Query 2D - Immutable
description: Given a 2D matrix matrix, handle multiple queries of the form: sumRegion(row1, col1, row2, col2) returns the sum of elements in the rectangle defined by (row1,col1) and (row2,col2).
constraints:
m == matrix.length
n == matrix[i].length
1 ≤ m, n ≤ 200
-10^4 ≤ matrix[i][j] ≤ 10^4
0 ≤ row1 ≤ row2 < m
0 ≤ col1 ≤ col2 < n
explanation: Sum of rectangle = 8 using prefix sums.
hints: Build 2D prefix sum array.
prefix[i][j] = sum of elements from (0,0) to (i-1,j-1).
sumRegion(r1,c1,r2,c2) = prefix[r2+1][c2+1] - prefix[r1][c2+1] - prefix[r2+1][c1] + prefix[r1][c1].
O(1) per query after O(mn) preprocessing.

title: Search a 2D Matrix
description: Write an efficient algorithm that searches for a value target in an m×n integer matrix. The matrix has: integers in each row sorted left to right, and each row's first integer greater than the last integer of the previous row.
constraints:
m == matrix.length
n == matrix[i].length
1 ≤ m, n ≤ 100
-10^4 ≤ matrix[i][j], target ≤ 10^4
explanation: 3 is found at position (0,1).
hints: Treat the 2D matrix as a 1D sorted array of size m*n.
Binary search: mid_val = matrix[mid/n][mid%n].
Adjust lo and hi based on comparison.
Total elements = m*n.

title: Maximum Product Subarray
description: Given an integer array nums, find a subarray that has the largest product and return the product.
constraints:
1 ≤ nums.length ≤ 2 * 10^4
-10 ≤ nums[i] ≤ 10
The product of any subarray fits in a 32-bit integer
explanation: Subarray [2,3] has max product 6.
hints: Track both maximum and minimum products ending at each position.
Negative * negative can become maximum.
At each step: maxProd = max(nums[i], maxProd*nums[i], minProd*nums[i]).
Similarly update minProd.

title: Bus Routes
description: You are given an array routes, where routes[i] is a bus route (visited stops). You start at source stop and want to reach target stop. You can take at most one bus at a time. Return the least number of buses you must take to travel from source to target, or -1.
constraints:
1 ≤ routes.length ≤ 500
1 ≤ routes[i].length ≤ 10^5
All values in routes[i] are unique
sum of routes[i].length ≤ 10^5
0 ≤ routes[i][j] < 10^6
0 ≤ source, target < 10^6
explanation: Take bus 0 (stops 1,2,7) to stop 7, then bus 1 (stops 3,6,7) to stop 6. 2 buses.
hints: BFS on bus routes, not stops.
Build map: stop → list of routes passing through it.
Start BFS from all routes containing source.
Visit neighboring routes reachable via shared stops.
Count route-level transitions (buses taken).

title: Trapping Rain Water II
description: Given an m×n integer matrix heightMap representing the height of each cell in a 2D elevation map, return the volume of water it can trap after raining.
constraints:
m == heightMap.length
n == heightMap[0].length
2 ≤ m, n ≤ 200
0 ≤ heightMap[i][j] ≤ 2 * 10^4
explanation: The inner cells can trap 4 units of water.
hints: Use a min-heap (priority queue) initialized with all border cells.
BFS-like: pop min height cell, explore neighbors.
Water trapped at a cell = max(0, current min height - cell height).
Mark visited cells.
Update min height for propagation: max(current min, neighbor height).

title: Replace Words
description: In English, we have a concept called root, which can be followed by some other word to form another longer word — let's call this word successor. Given a dictionary of roots and a sentence string, replace all successors in the sentence with the root. Return the modified sentence.
constraints:
1 ≤ dictionary.length ≤ 1000
1 ≤ dictionary[i].length ≤ 100
dictionary[i] consists of only lowercase letters
1 ≤ sentence.length ≤ 10^6
sentence consists of only lowercase letters and spaces
sampleInput: dictionary = ["cat","bat","rat"], sentence = "the cattle was rattled by the battery"
sampleOutput: "the cat was rat by the bat"
explanation: Each word is replaced by its shortest matching root prefix.
hints: Build a Trie from all roots.
For each word in sentence, traverse Trie character by character.
If a root (end-of-word) is found, replace with that root.
If no prefix matches, keep original word.
Join results back into sentence.

title: Minimum Number of Refueling Stops
description: A car travels from position 0 to a target destination. Along the way, there are gas stations. Given target, startFuel, and stations array where stations[i]=[position, fuel], return the minimum number of refueling stops needed. Return -1 if destination is not reachable.
constraints:
1 ≤ target, startFuel ≤ 10^9
0 ≤ stations.length ≤ 500
0 ≤ stations[0][0] < stations[1][0] < ... < target
1 ≤ stations[i][1] ≤ 10^9
explanation: Stop at station 0 (60 fuel), then station 3 (40 fuel). Total 2 stops.
hints: Greedy with max-heap: always refuel with largest available fuel when stuck.
Simulate driving; add stations to heap as you pass them.
When can't reach next station/target, take largest fuel from heap.
If heap is empty and still can't proceed, return -1.
Count heap pops = refueling stops.

title: Maximum Sum of 3 Non-Overlapping Subarrays
description: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, find three non-overlapping subarrays of length k with maximum sum and return their starting indices. If multiple answers, return the lexicographically smallest one.
hints: Compute window sums of length k.
Precompute left[i] = index of max sum window in [0..i].
Precompute right[i] = index of max sum window in [i..n-k].
Iterate middle window from k to n-2k.
For each middle j, check left[j-1] and right[j+k] to maximize total.

title: Power of Two
description: Given an integer n, return true if it is a power of two. Otherwise, return false.
explanation: 16 = 2^4, so it is a power of two.

title: Invert Binary Tree
description: Given the root of a binary tree, invert the tree and return its root.
constraints:
The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 100]
-100 ≤ Node.val ≤ 100
explanation: Swap left and right children at every node.

title: Sum of Two Integers
description: Given two integers a and b, return the sum without using the operators + and -.
explanation: Use XOR for sum bits, AND shifted left for carry.
hints: XOR gives sum without carry.
AND gives positions of carry bits.
Shift carry left by 1 and add recursively.
Repeat until carry is 0.
Handle negative numbers with 32-bit mask.

title: Find Peak Element
description: A peak element is an element that is strictly greater than its neighbors. Given a 0-indexed integer array nums, find a peak element and return its index. The array may contain multiple peaks; return any peak index. Must run in O(log n).
constraints:
1 ≤ nums.length ≤ 1000
-2^31 ≤ nums[i] ≤ 2^31 - 1
nums[i] != nums[i+1] for all valid i
explanation: Element 3 at index 2 is a peak (greater than both neighbors).
hints: Binary search: mid is a peak if nums[mid]>nums[mid-1] and nums[mid]>nums[mid+1].
If nums[mid] < nums[mid+1], peak is in right half.
Otherwise peak is in left half (including mid).
Always move toward the larger neighbor.

title: Copy List with Random Pointer
description: A linked list of length n is given where each node has an additional random pointer that could point to any node in the list or null. Return a deep copy of the list.
constraints:
0 ≤ n ≤ 1000
-10^4 ≤ Node.val ≤ 10^4
Node.random is null or pointing to some node in the linked list
explanation: A deep copy with new nodes but same relative random pointer structure.
hints: Use a HashMap to map original nodes to their copies.
First pass: create all new nodes.
Second pass: set next and random pointers using the map.

title: Maximum Profit in Job Scheduling
description: We have n jobs. Each job i has startTime[i], endTime[i], and profit[i]. You want to find the subset of jobs with non-overlapping time ranges that gives maximum profit.
constraints:
1 ≤ startTime.length == endTime.length == profit.length ≤ 5 * 10^4
1 ≤ startTime[i] < endTime[i] ≤ 10^9
1 ≤ profit[i] ≤ 10^4
explanation: Jobs (1→3, profit 50) and (3→6, profit 70) are non-overlapping. Total=120.
hints: Sort jobs by end time.
dp[i] = max profit using first i jobs.
For each job i, binary search for latest non-overlapping job j.
dp[i] = max(dp[i-1], profit[i] + dp[j]).
Use coordinate compression or binary search on end times.

title: Minimum Number of Taps to Open to Water a Garden
description: There is a garden with n+1 positions labeled 0 to n. In each position i, there is a tap with range ranges[i] meaning it can water [i-ranges[i], i+ranges[i]]. Return the minimum number of taps that should be open to water the whole garden from 0 to n. Return -1 if impossible.
explanation: Tap at position 1 with range 4 covers [−3,5], which covers [0,5]. Only 1 tap needed.
hints: Convert to interval covering problem.
For each tap i: interval = [i-ranges[i], i+ranges[i]].
Sort intervals by start position.
Greedy jump game: maximize right reach from current coverage.
Similar to Jump Game II.

title: Swim in Rising Water
description: You are given an n×n grid where grid[i][j] represents the elevation at (i,j). At time t, every position with elevation ≤ t is underwater. You can swim to adjacent 4-directional cells. Find the minimum time t such that you can swim from (0,0) to (n-1,n-1).
constraints:
n == grid.length
n == grid[i].length
1 ≤ n ≤ 50
0 ≤ grid[i][j] < n^2
All values of grid are unique
explanation: At time t=3, all cells with elevation ≤ 3 are reachable.
hints: Use Dijkstra's / modified BFS.
State = (max elevation seen on path so far, row, col).
Min-heap: pop cell with minimum bottleneck elevation.
Relax neighbors: new cost = max(current max, neighbor elevation).
Answer = first time we pop (n-1,n-1).

title: 24 Game
description: You are given an integer array cards of length 4. You have four cards, each containing a number. You can add, subtract, multiply, or divide, use each card exactly once. Return true if you can get exactly 24.
hints: Try all permutations of 4 numbers.
Try all combinations of 3 operators (+,-,*,/).
Try all ways to parenthesize (5 tree structures for 4 numbers).
Recursively: pick two numbers, apply one operator, replace with result, recurse.
Use floating point with epsilon comparison.

title: Regions Cut by Slashes

constraints:
n == grid.length
1 ≤ n ≤ 30
grid[i][j] is either '/', '\', or ' '
explanation: The slashes divide the grid into 4 regions.
hints: Upscale each cell to 3×3 grid.
Draw slashes as walls in the 3×3 representation.
Use Union-Find or DFS to count connected regions.
Alternative: divide each cell into 4 triangles, union based on slash type.
Count connected components after processing all cells.

title: Majority Element

explanation: 2 appears 4 times in an array of size 7.

title: Merge Two Sorted Lists

constraints:
The number of nodes in both lists is in the range [0, 50]
-100 ≤ Node.val ≤ 100
Both lists are sorted in non-decreasing order
explanation: Merged sorted list of all elements.

title: Network Delay Time
description: You are given a network of n nodes (labeled 1 to n) and times, a list of travel times [u,v,w] representing directed edges. Find the minimum time for all n nodes to receive the signal sent from k. Return -1 if impossible.

1 ≤ k ≤ n ≤ 100
1 ≤ times.length ≤ 6000
times[i].length == 3
1 ≤ u_i, v_i ≤ n
u_i != v_i

explanation: Dijkstra from node 2: max shortest path = 2.

Track shortest distance to all nodes.
Return max distance; if any node unreachable, return -1.
Use a min-heap for efficient relaxation.

title: Unique Paths II
description: A robot starts at (0,0) on an m×n grid and needs to reach (m-1,n-1). The grid contains obstacles (1=obstacle, 0=empty). Count unique paths avoiding obstacles.
constraints:
m == obstacleGrid.length
n == obstacleGrid[0].length

obstacleGrid[i][j] is 0 or 1
explanation: The center obstacle blocks some paths; 2 valid paths remain.

dp[i][j] = 0 if obstacle.
dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j] + dp[i][j-1] otherwise.
Handle first row and column separately.

title: K Closest Points to Origin
description: Given an array of points where points[i]=[xi, yi] and an integer k, return the k closest points to the origin (0,0). The answer is guaranteed to be unique except for the order.

Add points by distance; if heap exceeds k, remove farthest.

title: Largest Rectangle in Histogram
description: Given an array of integers heights representing the histogram's bar height where the width of each bar is 1, return the area of the largest rectangle in the histogram.
explanation: The largest rectangle is formed by bars of height 5 and 6, area = 5*2 = 10.
hints: Use a monotonic stack.
For each bar, find left and right boundaries where bar is the minimum.
Area = height * (right_boundary - left_boundary - 1).
Push indices onto stack; when a bar is shorter than stack top, pop and compute.
Append 0-height bar at end to flush stack.

title: Word Ladder II
description: Given two words beginWord and endWord, and a dictionary wordList, find all shortest transformation sequences from beginWord to endWord, such that each transformed word exists in wordList. Return all sequences.
constraints:
1 ≤ beginWord.length ≤ 5
endWord.length == beginWord.length
1 ≤ wordList.length ≤ 500
wordList[i].length == beginWord.length
All words consist of lowercase English letters
All words are unique
explanation: Two shortest paths of length 5 exist.
hints: BFS to find shortest path length and build parent graph.
DFS/backtracking to reconstruct all paths from parent graph.
At each BFS level, track all words at same distance.
Don't revisit words from previous levels.
Build neighbor function: try all single-char changes.

title: Max Consecutive Ones III
description: Given a binary array nums and an integer k, return the maximum number of consecutive 1's in the array if you can flip at most k 0's.
constraints:

nums[i] is either 0 or 1
0 ≤ k ≤ nums.length
explanation: Flip two 0's at positions 9 and 10 (0-indexed) for a run of 6.

If zeros > k, shrink window from left.
Track maximum window size where zeros ≤ k.
Window represents the longest valid subarray.

title: Satisfiability of Equality Equations
description: You are given an array of strings equations where each equation[i] is a string of length 4 with format 'a==b' or 'a!=b'. Return true if all given equations can be satisfied simultaneously.
constraints:

equations[i].length == 4

equations[i][1] is either '=' or '!'
equations[i][2] is '='
explanation: a==b implies a and b are in same set, but b!=a contradicts this.
hints: Process all '==' equations first using Union-Find.
Then check all '!=' equations.
If a!=b but find(a)==find(b), return false.
Union find with path compression for efficiency.
Map characters to indices 0-25.

title: Generate Parentheses
description: Given n pairs of parentheses, write a function to generate all combinations of well-formed parentheses.
explanation: All valid 3-pair parentheses combinations.
hints: Backtracking with open and close counts.
Add '(' if open < n.
Add ')' if close < open.
Base: when length == 2n, add to result.
Prune invalid paths early.

title: Path Sum
description: Given the root of a binary tree and an integer targetSum, return true if the tree has a root-to-leaf path such that adding up all the values along the path equals targetSum.
constraints:
The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 5000]
-1000 ≤ Node.val ≤ 1000
-1000 ≤ targetSum ≤ 1000
explanation: Path 5→4→11→2 sums to 22.

title: Longest Common Prefix

constraints:
1 ≤ strs.length ≤ 200
0 ≤ strs[i].length ≤ 200
strs[i] consists of only lowercase English letters
explanation: Common prefix of all three strings is 'fl'.

title: Permutation in String
description: Given two strings s1 and s2, return true if s2 contains a permutation of s1, or false otherwise. In other words, return true if one of s1's permutations is a substring of s2.
constraints:

s1 and s2 consist of lowercase English letters
explanation: s2 contains 'ba' which is a permutation of 'ab'.
hints: Sliding window of length s1.length over s2.
Compare character frequency maps.
Update window by adding right char and removing left char.
Use an array of 26 counts for efficiency.

title: LRU Cache
description: Design a data structure that follows the Least Recently Used (LRU) cache constraint. Implement the LRUCache class: get(key) returns value or -1; put(key, value) inserts or updates key. If capacity exceeded, evict least recently used.
constraints:
1 ≤ capacity ≤ 3000
0 ≤ key ≤ 10^4
0 ≤ value ≤ 10^5
At most 2 * 10^5 calls to get and put
sampleInput: capacity=2, put(1,1), put(2,2), get(1), put(3,3), get(2)
explanation: After put(3,3), key 2 is evicted (LRU). get(2) returns -1.
hints: Use doubly linked list + HashMap.
HashMap: key → node for O(1) access.
Doubly linked list: maintain order of use.
On get/put: move accessed node to front.
On eviction: remove from back of list.

title: Time Based Key-Value Store
description: Design a time-based key-value data structure. set(key, value, timestamp) stores key at given timestamp. get(key, timestamp) returns value with the largest timestamp ≤ given timestamp, or empty string.
constraints:

hints: HashMap: key → list of (timestamp, value) pairs.
Timestamps in list are already sorted (strictly increasing).
Use binary search on timestamp list for get.
Find largest timestamp ≤ query timestamp using bisect.

title: Cheapest Flights Within K Stops
description: There are n cities connected by flights. Given an array flights=[from,to,price], find the cheapest price from src to dst with at most k stops. Return -1 if no route.
constraints:

hints: Use Bellman-Ford relaxed to k+1 iterations.
dp[i][v] = min cost to reach v using at most i edges.
For each step, only use previous step's costs (not current iteration).
Copy previous state before each iteration.
Answer = dp[k+1][dst].

title: Minimum Path Sum in Triangle
description: Given a triangle array, return the minimum path sum from top to bottom. Each step you may move to an adjacent number on the row below. Use O(n) extra space.
hints: Start from the bottom row.
For each cell, add the minimum of the two choices below.
dp[j] = triangle[i][j] + min(dp[j], dp[j+1]).
Work bottom-up modifying dp in place.
Return dp[0] after processing all rows.

title: Word Break II
description: Given a string s and a dictionary of strings wordDict, add spaces in s to construct a sentence where each word is a valid dictionary word. Return all such possible sentences in any order.
constraints:
1 ≤ s.length ≤ 20
1 ≤ wordDict.length ≤ 1000
1 ≤ wordDict[i].length ≤ 10
s and wordDict[i] consist of only lowercase English letters
All strings in wordDict are unique
explanation: Two ways to break s into valid dictionary words.
hints: Backtracking with memoization.
At each position, try all words that match current prefix.
Recurse from position after matched word.
Memoize: memo[i] = all sentences from position i to end.
Combine current word with results from memo.

title: Merge K Sorted Lists
description: You are given an array of k linked-lists lists, each sorted in ascending order. Merge all the linked-lists into one sorted linked-list and return it.
constraints:

lists[i] is sorted in ascending order

explanation: All three lists merged into one sorted list.
hints: Use a min-heap of size k.
Initialize heap with first node of each list.
Pop minimum, add to result, push its next node.
Continue until heap is empty.
Use (val, index, node) tuples to avoid comparison issues.

title: Range Minimum Query
description: Build a data structure that, given an integer array, efficiently answers multiple queries of the form: what is the minimum value in range [l, r]? Support point updates.
constraints:

-10^4 ≤ nums[i] ≤ 10^4
At most 3 * 10^4 queries and updates
hints: Build a segment tree storing minimum values.

Update: O(log n) traversal from leaf to root.
Query: combine relevant segments.
Each query/update takes O(log n).

title: Detect Capital

constraints:

word consists of uppercase and lowercase English letters
explanation: All letters are capitals, which is correct.

title: Find Pivot Index
description: Given an integer array nums, calculate the pivot index where the sum of all the numbers strictly to the left of the index is equal to the sum to the right.
explanation: At index 3, left sum = 1+7+3=11, right sum = 5+6=11.

title: Partition Equal Subset Sum
description: Given an integer array nums, return true if you can partition the array into two subsets such that the sum of the elements in both subsets is equal.
explanation: Split into [1,5,5] and [11], both sum to 11.
hints: If total sum is odd, return false.
Target = total/2; solve 0/1 knapsack for that target.
dp[j] = true if subset sum j is achievable.
For each num, update dp from right: dp[j] |= dp[j-num].
Return dp[target].

title: Validate Binary Search Tree
description: Given the root of a binary tree, determine if it is a valid binary search tree (BST). A BST is valid if left subtree < node < right subtree for every node.
constraints:

-2^31 ≤ Node.val ≤ 2^31 - 1
explanation: Left child 1 < 2 < right child 3. Valid BST.
hints: Use min/max boundary propagation.
Each node must be in range (min, max).

Right child: range (node.val, max).
Initialize with (-inf, +inf).

title: Gas Station
description: There are n gas stations along a circular route. gas[i] is the amount of gas at the ith station, and cost[i] is the cost to travel from station i to the next. Return the starting station index if you can travel around the circuit once, or -1 if impossible.
constraints:
n == gas.length == cost.length
1 ≤ n ≤ 10^5
0 ≤ gas[i], cost[i] ≤ 10^4
explanation: Start at station 3 (gas 4), can complete the circuit.
hints: If total gas < total cost, no solution.
Greedily find start: reset start when tank goes negative.
Tank = sum of (gas[i]-cost[i]); if < 0, reset start to i+1.
After one pass, return start.
This works because if a solution exists, it's unique.

title: Integer Break
description: Given an integer n, break it into the sum of k positive integers (k ≥ 2) and maximize the product of those integers. Return the maximum product.
hints: dp[i] = max product for integer i.
For each j from 1 to i-1: dp[i] = max(j*(i-j), j*dp[i-j]).
j*(i-j) means split into just two parts.
j*dp[i-j] means split further.
Greedy: break into 3s whenever possible (mathematical proof).

title: Minimum Height Trees
description: A tree of n nodes labeled 0 to n-1 is given. For each node as root, we get a rooted tree with some height. Return all nodes that can be roots of minimum height trees.

edges.length == n - 1

ai != bi

title: Maximum Frequency Stack

Map: frequency → stack of elements at that frequency.

title: Maximum XOR With an Element From Array

Sort nums.
Process queries in increasing mi order, adding eligible nums to Trie.

title: Beautiful Arrangement
description: Suppose you have n integers labeled 1 through n. A permutation of those integers is considered beautiful if for every i (1≤i≤n), either perm[i] is divisible by i or i is divisible by perm[i]. Return the number of beautiful arrangements.
hints: Backtracking: assign numbers to positions 1 to n.
At each position, try all unused numbers.

Count valid complete arrangements.

title: Maximum Subarray

title: Binary Tree Level Order Traversal

The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 2000]
-1000 ≤ Node.val ≤ 1000

For each city, count cities reachable within threshold.
Return city with minimum reachable count (or largest index on tie).

title: Find the Middle Index in Array

Iterate with running left sum.
At each index i: right sum = total - left sum - nums[i].
If left == right, return i.
Update left sum after check.

description: A conveyor belt has packages that must be shipped from one port to another within d days. The ith package has a weight of weights[i]. Each day, load packages in order until total weight exceeds ship capacity. Find the minimum capacity to ship all packages within d days.
hints: Binary search on capacity.
Low = max(weights), High = sum(weights).
For each mid capacity, simulate days needed.
If days ≤ d, reduce capacity; else increase.
Return minimum feasible capacity.

title: Minimum Cost For Tickets
description: You have planned travels on specific days in days array. You can buy 1-day passes ($costs[0]), 7-day passes ($costs[1]), or 30-day passes ($costs[2]). Return the minimum cost to travel every day in days.

costs.length == 3

explanation: Buy 1-day pass on day 1 (2), 7-day on day 4 (7), 1-day on day 20 (2). Total=11.

For 30-day pass: dp[i] = dp[max(0,i-30)] + costs[2].

title: Minimum Score of a Path Between Two Cities

roads[i].length == 3

explanation: Path 1→2→4 includes edge (2,4,5) with distance 5, the minimum on any path from 1 to n.

Track minimum edge seen among all edges in the component.
Cycles allow reusing edges; any edge in the component is accessible.

title: Reorganize String
description: Given a string s, rearrange the characters so that no two adjacent characters are the same. Return any possible rearrangement, or empty string if not possible.
constraints:

s consists of lowercase English letters
explanation: Rearrange so no two adjacent chars are the same.

If any char appears more than (n+1)/2 times, impossible.
Use max-heap on frequencies.
Each step: pick top two most frequent chars, add both.
Handle last remaining character if heap has one element.

description: Find all valid combinations of k numbers that sum up to n such that: only numbers 1 through 9 are used, each number is used at most once. Return a list of all possible valid combinations.

Pick k numbers summing to n.

title: Largest Component Size by Common Factor

Count component sizes using Union-Find.

title: First Unique Character in a String

Slide: add new, remove old, check sum >= k*threshold.
Count valid windows.

description: Design a data structure that supports addWord and search. search(word) returns true if any string in the data structure matches word where '.' can match any letter.

n == grid[i].length

grid[i][j] is either 0 or 1

Return maximum over all 0 cells (or max island if no 0 exists).

title: Falling Squares
description: There are several squares being dropped onto the X-axis of a 2D plane. Given a 2D integer array positions where positions[i]=[left_i, sideLength_i], return an integer array ans where ans[i] represents the height of the tallest stack of squares after dropping the ith square.
explanation: Square 1: height 2. Square 2 lands on square 1: height 5. Square 3: height 1, tallest still 5.
hints: For each new square [l,s], it lands on top of current max height in range [l,l+s).
New square's top = current max in range + s.
Update range [l,l+s) to new top.
Use segment tree with range max query and range update.
Coordinate compress positions for efficiency.

title: Diameter of Binary Tree
description: Given the root of a binary tree, return the length of the diameter of the tree. The diameter is the length of the longest path between any two nodes (may or may not pass through root).
constraints:
The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [1, 10^4]
-100 ≤ Node.val ≤ 100
explanation: Longest path is 4→2→1→3 or 5→2→1→3 with length 3.

title: Rotate Array
description: Given an integer array nums, rotate the array to the right by k steps, where k is non-negative. Do this in-place.
explanation: Rotate 3 steps right: last 3 elements move to front.

title: Encode and Decode Strings
description: Design an algorithm to encode a list of strings to a single string. The encoded string is then sent over the network and is decoded back to the original list of strings.
constraints:
1 ≤ strs.length ≤ 200
0 ≤ strs[i].length ≤ 200
strs[i] contains any possible characters out of 256 valid ASCII characters
explanation: Encode to single string and decode back to original list.
hints: Prefix each string with its length followed by a delimiter.
Example: '4#lint4#code4#love3#you'.
During decode, read length, skip delimiter, read that many chars.
This handles any characters including special chars.
Repeat until end of encoded string.

title: Koko Eating Bananas
description: Koko loves bananas. There are n piles of bananas. Koko can decide her eating speed k (bananas/hr). Each hour she picks a pile and eats min(k, pile) bananas. Return the minimum integer k such that she can eat all bananas within h hours.
explanation: Speed 4: ceil(3/4)+ceil(6/4)+ceil(7/4)+ceil(11/4)=1+2+2+3=8 hours.
hints: Binary search on speed k.
Low=1, High=max(piles).
For each mid speed, compute total hours needed.
If hours ≤ h, try smaller speed; else try larger.
Return minimum feasible speed.

title: Reorder List
description: You are given the head of a singly linked-list L0→L1→…→Ln-1→Ln. Reorder it to: L0→Ln→L1→Ln-1→L2→Ln-2→... You may not modify the values in the list's nodes.
constraints:
The number of nodes in the list is in the range [1, 5 * 10^4]
1 ≤ Node.val ≤ 1000
explanation: Interleave from both ends: 1,5,2,4,3.
hints: Find middle using slow/fast pointers.
Reverse second half of list.
Merge two halves by interleaving.
Handle edge cases for odd/even length.

title: Edit Distance
description: Given two strings word1 and word2, return the minimum number of operations (insert, delete, replace) required to convert word1 to word2.
constraints:
0 ≤ word1.length, word2.length ≤ 500
word1 and word2 consist of lowercase English letters
explanation: horse→rorse (replace h→r)→rose (remove r)→ros (remove e). 3 operations.
hints: dp[i][j] = min operations to convert word1[0..i-1] to word2[0..j-1].
If chars match: dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j-1].
Else: dp[i][j] = 1 + min(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1], dp[i-1][j-1]).
Base: dp[i][0]=i, dp[0][j]=j.
Fill table row by row.

title: Shortest Path Visiting All Nodes
description: You have an undirected, connected graph of n labeled nodes. Given the graph as adjacency list, return the length of the shortest path that visits every node. You may start and stop at any node, revisit nodes, and revisit edges.
constraints:
n == graph.length
1 ≤ n ≤ 12
0 ≤ graph[i].length < n
There are no self-loops
The input graph is always connected
explanation: Visit 1→0→2→0→3, total length 4.
hints: BFS with bitmask state: (node, visited_bitmask).
Initial states: all nodes with their bit set (can start anywhere).
State: (current_node, bitmask_of_visited_nodes).
Goal: bitmask == (1<<n)-1 (all nodes visited).
Use visited set to avoid revisiting same (node, mask) pair.

title: Kth Largest Element in a Stream
description: Design a class to find the kth largest element in a stream. Implement KthLargest(k, nums) constructor and add(val) that returns the kth largest element after each addition.
constraints:
1 ≤ k ≤ 10^4
0 ≤ nums.length ≤ 10^4
-10^4 ≤ nums[i] ≤ 10^4
-10^4 ≤ val ≤ 10^4
At most 10^4 calls to add
It is guaranteed that there will be at least k elements in the array when you search for the kth element
explanation: Min-heap of size k maintains the k largest elements.
hints: Use a min-heap of size k.
Top of heap = kth largest.
On add: push val; if heap size > k, pop minimum.
Return heap top.
Initialize heap with nums, then trim to size k.

title: Restore IP Addresses
description: A valid IP address consists of exactly four integers separated by single dots. Each integer is between 0 and 255 and cannot have leading zeros. Given a string s containing only digits, return all possible valid IP addresses.
constraints:
1 ≤ s.length ≤ 20
s consists of digits only
explanation: Two valid IP addresses can be formed.
hints: Backtracking: place dots at 3 positions.
Each segment must be 1-3 digits, value 0-255.
No leading zeros (except '0' itself).
When 4 segments and all chars used, record result.
Prune if remaining chars can't form valid segments.

title: Making a Minimum Spanning Tree
description: Given a graph of n nodes and edges with weights, find the minimum spanning tree weight using Kruskal's algorithm. Return the total weight of the MST, or -1 if the graph is disconnected.
constraints:
2 ≤ n ≤ 10^4
1 ≤ edges.length ≤ 10^5
edges[i] = [u, v, weight]
0 ≤ u, v < n
1 ≤ weight ≤ 1000
explanation: MST uses edges (0,1,1),(2,3,1),(1,2,2) with total weight 4.
hints: Sort edges by weight.
Use Union-Find to detect cycles.
Add edge if it connects two different components.
Stop when n-1 edges are added.
If fewer than n-1 edges added, return -1.

title: Reverse String II
description: Given a string s and an integer k, reverse the first k characters for every 2k characters counting from the start of the string. If fewer than k characters remain, reverse all of them.
constraints:
1 ≤ s.length ≤ 10^4
s consists of only lowercase English letters
1 ≤ k ≤ 10^4
explanation: Reverse first 2 chars of every 4: 'ab'→'ba', 'ef'→'fe', leaving 'g' unchanged.

title: Implement Stack using Queues
description: Implement a last-in-first-out (LIFO) stack using only two queues. Implement push, pop, top, and empty operations.
constraints:
1 ≤ x ≤ 9
At most 100 calls to push, pop, top, and empty
All calls to pop and top are valid
explanation: Stack: last pushed = first out.

title: Boats to Save People
description: You are given an array people where people[i] is the weight of the ith person, and an infinite number of boats where each boat can carry a maximum weight of limit. Each boat carries at most 2 people. Return the minimum number of boats to carry every person.
hints: Sort people by weight.
Two pointers: heaviest and lightest.
If they fit together, pair them (both pointers move).
Otherwise, heaviest goes alone (right pointer moves left).
Count boats.

title: Climbing Stairs
description: You are climbing a staircase. It takes n steps to reach the top. Each time you can either climb 1 or 2 steps. In how many distinct ways can you climb to the top?
hints: dp[i] = ways to reach step i.
dp[i] = dp[i-1] + dp[i-2].
Fibonacci-like sequence.

title: Binary Tree Right Side View
